ops.text: PRE-REGISTRATION E31 (Wave 0; cheap analysis, feeds E40's baseline + labels). Hypothesis: the user's steering is NOT uniform over a session — steering probability varies systematically by position-in-session × dimension (category), forming a "demand curve" for clarification/correction that a position-prior heuristic can exploit (and that E40's imminence watcher must beat). Data ($0-mostly, $5 cap for any optional labeling): frozen decisions.parquet (2055 rounds, digest 219ea34f) + trajectory export (4014 rows, 522 steer turns; from db4c6914) + the steering anchors in the traces corpus. Method, computed once: (1) bucket every turn by normalized session position (deciles of turn_index/session_length) × steering dimension (the 11-way category, or the 6 steering categories); (2) steering rate per bucket; (3) test against a uniform-null (χ² over position deciles; per-category position distributions); (4) session-clustered bootstrap CI on the position effect. Deliverable: the position×dimension demand curve + a POSITION-PRIOR HEURISTIC (predict steer-imminence from position alone) with its AUC on the trajectory frame — this is E40's baseline-to-beat (E40's observed-future watcher must beat the position prior, per the plan). Pre-registered reads: strong position effect → the prior is a real baseline E40 must clear; flat → steering is position-agnostic and E40 has a lower bar. Harness ~/.cc-steer/experiments/E31/ own venv. VERIFY: independent recompute of the bucket rates + the prior's AUC from the persisted trajectory + decisions artifacts.
